title: Parallel Tool Calls, TaskGroup Ownership, Ordering, and Shared Effects · Language Clinic: Parallel Cleanup
summary: The agent launches every call concurrently, loses call-result order, mutates shared session state, swallows ExceptionGroup failures, and leaks tasks after cancellation. Predict authority, model and API behavior, state, tool effects, budgets, privacy, safety, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the parallel cleanup increment in structured bounded parallel tool executor. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, c…
objectives: Cancel siblings when policy requires, collect partial outcomes, close handlers, reconcile uncertain effects, and prove no task survives the turn.
Serialize or transactionally coordinate calls that touch shared resources, quotas, sessions, files, records, or external effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-worked-agentpy-parallel-tool-calls-3
# Competency: agentpy-worked-agentpy-parallel-tool-calls-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review.
# Operating constraint: every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy.
# Changed case: change one tool call, argument, authority, result, or failure and preserve correlation.
def worked_agentpy_parallel_tool_calls_3(scheduled_calls: int = 12, completed_calls: int = 12, parallel_limit: int = 4, peak_parallel: int = 3, leaked_tasks: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorityworked3",
        "interaction5798",
        "tool3dekrow",
        "stateworked35798",
        "evaluation5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if scheduled_calls < 0 or completed_calls < 0:
        return False, completed_calls, "parallel-count"
    if completed_calls != scheduled_calls:
        return False, completed_calls, "parallel-reconciliation"
    if parallel_limit < 1 or peak_parallel > parallel_limit:
        return False, peak_parallel, "parallel-budget"
    if leaked_tasks > 0:
        return False, leaked_tasks, "task-leak"
    assert completed_calls == scheduled_calls and leaked_tasks == 0
    return True, completed_calls, "structured-tool-concurrency"

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-parallel-cleanup-15rjtp
# Competency: agentpy-parallel-cleanup.
# Case 7911: a clean package, canary, and recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: telemetry and memory must remain tenant-isolated, minimized, redacted, and attributable.

    assert evidence_variant_15rjtp
    evidence_axes_15rjtp = (
        "authority15rjtp",
        "interaction7911",
        "toolptjr51",
        "state15rjtp7911",
        "evaluation791115rjtp",
        "recoveryptjr517911",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_15rjtp)) == 6
    if scheduled_calls < 0 or completed_calls < 0:
        return False, completed_calls, "parallel-count"
    if completed_calls != scheduled_calls:
        return False, completed_calls, "parallel-reconciliation"
    if parallel_limit < 1 or peak_parallel > parallel_limit:
        return False, peak_parallel, "parallel-budget"
    if leaked_tasks > 0:
        return False, leaked_tasks, "task-leak"
    assert completed_calls == scheduled_calls and leaked_tasks == 0
    return True, completed_calls, "structured-tool-concurrency"
